subject: Geography
title: Carlsbad Caverns
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Carlsbad Caverns.

hook.text: Every summer evening, 400,000 bats spiral out of Carlsbad Caverns in a living tornado.

buildup.text: The cave's Big Room is the largest natural chamber in North America—big enough for six football fields.

discovery.text: Cowboys first found the cave by following the bat swarm, thinking it was smoke from a fire.

twist.text: Early 'explorers' mined tons of bat guano for fertilizer before it became a national park.

climax.text: Carlsbad shows how caves can be both fragile ecosystems and jaw-dropping spectacles.

punchline.text: Cowboys followed smoke into one of Earth's greatest caverns.

quiz.question: How were Carlsbad Caverns first discovered by cowboys?
options: They followed a bat swarm they mistook for smoke
